title: From Packing List to Trackside Operations
subtitle: How repeated drift events turned a simple packing list into a better way to prepare.
summary: A firsthand field guide to planning tires, vehicle support, paddock logistics, weather protection, cleanup, communication, and recovery for a complete drift day.
excerpt: My drift-day packing list did not come together all at once. It grew through repeated events, when small oversights between sessions became harder to ignore and the difference between being prepared and overpacking became clearer.

title: From Scrolling Résumé to Navigable Interface
subtitle: What I learned by transforming a conventional card-based portfolio into a compact, layered professional system.
summary: The original portfolio was polished but conventional. Rebuilding it around layered navigation, progressive disclosure, and content-specific layouts changed it from a scrolling résumé into a more deliberate professional interface.
excerpt: My original portfolio was functional, readable, responsive, and professionally presentable. It also gave me something more valuable than a polished mockup: a real system I could use and evaluate.

title: Performance Starts Before the Start Line
subtitle: How sleep, nutrition, movement, warm-up, and mindset matter before a competitive driving event.
summary: A practical field note on reducing avoidable performance loss by preparing the driver as deliberately as the vehicle.
excerpt: A driver can arrive with a prepared car and still perform below their ability. Sleep, nutrition, movement, warm-up, and mental preparation influence consistency long before the first competitive run begins.

title: When AI Starts Competing With Games
subtitle: AI can give people content to watch, agents to direct, and tools that make creation feel like entertainment.
summary: AI may compete with games by offering three forms of entertainment: generated media to consume, intelligent systems to direct, and tools that make creation feel like play.
excerpt: Lately, when I have a free hour, I am less likely to start a game. I am more likely to open ChatGPT or Codex, direct several agents, test what they changed, and decide what to improve next.
